title: ageratina espinosarum
intro: Ageratina espinosarum, also known as Hierba del Perro, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the mint family Lamiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The plant is rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. Ageratina espinosarum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
